返回本批次

多市场赌徒在战争上投注数百万美元

Polymarket gamblers betting millions on war

www.theguardian.com 作者 sandebert 分数 151 评论 120 2026/04/11 20:28
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. “霍雷昆登”很快就失去了耐心。他对战争研究所感到沮丧，这是一家美国智库，每天制作乌克兰前线地图。对于霍雷昆登和其他匿名赌徒来说，这张地图是“脱节、不连贯的混乱……就像一个五岁孩子的画”。因此，他们的目标是在在线预测市场 Polymarket 上下注，这对他们来说没有任何用处。他们不满意的地图描绘了 Kostyantynivka 市，乌克兰军队在炮击和无人机群中占领了该市五个月。数千名平民仍然居住在那里。目前，俄罗斯今年是否会占领科斯蒂安提尼夫卡的赌注已超过 500,000 美元（371,000 英镑）。如果 ISW 发布一张显示俄罗斯占领该市火车站的地图，这些赌注就会尘埃落定。 在蓬勃发展的在线经济中，成千上万的人正在讨论如何从战争中获利。他们把赌注押在乌克兰身上。他们押注于美伊停火——赌注为 2.8 亿美元。他们押注美国是否会入侵伊朗——赌注为 750 万美元。一名乌克兰军人行走在乌克兰科斯特扬尼夫卡的住宅楼废墟中。 Polymarket 用户已经打赌俄罗斯是否会占领这座城市。照片：Oleg Petrasiuk/乌克兰武装部队第 24 机械化旅/法新社/Getty Images 在消息应用程序 Discord 主办的小组中，他们讨论可能发生的事情。一位对美国是否会袭击伊朗哈格岛石油码头的用户写道：“糟糕透了。第三次世界大战可能会发生。”另一位用户写道，“只要美国获得伊朗的铀，我就可以看到上述所有和平都会实现。”他似乎在赌本周的停火。有时，他们似乎正在合作影响事件的进程，或者至少影响事件的记录方式。批评者称这是不道德的。美国参议员呼吁对 Polymarket 进行监管。然而，Polymarket 将自己视为事实的来源。几周前，有报道称其似乎正在对美国对伊朗的打击进行内幕押注，该公司发布了一份“关于中东市场的说明”。“在与那些直接受到袭击影响的人进行讨论后，他们提出了数十个问题，我们意识到预测市场可以以电视新闻和 X 无法提供的方式为他们提供所需的答案，”它说。Polymarket 的崛起和崛起2024 年 7 月，唐纳德·特朗普在美国再次当选前不久，Polymarket 报告称，当年其平台上的交易总额约为 4 亿美元。现在它可以在一天之内上涨。它称自己为“预测市场”——一种通过向公众提供押注方式来收集未来数据的方式。但长期用户表示，它越来越像一个赌场——从特朗普的愤怒到耶稣基督的第二次降临，一切都可以货币化。因此，只要有可能，这个强大的“赌场”中的玩家就开始尝试塑造世界，以确保发薪日。2024 年 7 月，Polymarket 平台上的交易额约为 4 亿美元。照片：Abaca Press/AlamyPolymarket 赌徒最近威胁一名以色列记者，要求他修改稿件，这样他们就可以赢得关于伊朗是否在某一天袭击以色列的赌注。专家表示，Polymarket 上的用户可能能够操纵更广泛的市场，其影响可能会波及机构和养老基金。 至于乌克兰前线，在 Discord 频道上吐槽 ISW 的地图后，Horekunden 决定采取更进一步的行动。他向另一位用户 @tsybka 发出了呼吁，他的 X 个人资料将他描述为平台上“排名前 0.001% 的交易者”。 Horekunden 写道：“您之前曾通过外展联系过他们，所以如果您能尝试将此事传递出去，我将不胜感激。”@tsybka 回答道，“这是无稽之谈，他们不会听任何人的。”他没有回复《卫报》的询问。“无论如何，这张地图都太糟糕了。”太不可思议了，”他写道。然后他决定亲自给 ISW 打电话。 “不幸的是，我们无能为力，因为所有其他地图制作者太容易贿赂了。”ISW 坚称，它“没有与此类平台的用户或代表接触”。一位发言人表示：“我们强烈谴责并从未同意利用我们的产品来达到赌博战争的可憎目的。”“没有人想从战争或死亡中赚钱，但是……”“我喜欢赌博，”约瑟夫·弗兰西亚说。 30 多岁的弗朗西娅一边在伯克利学习经济学，一边在赌场数牌，周末在内华达州里诺玩二十一点。他不是一个寻求刺激的“Yolo”（生命只有一次）赌徒，他说：他喜欢在庄家有优势的时候下注。在大学时，他和一个朋友决定从一些离岸体育博彩公司收集数据，并开始进行套利投注：利用不同投注网站给出的赔率差异进行投注。另一个网站，你可以打赌基本上，两支球队都在不同的体育博彩上，并有保证的利润。”他说。这个项目在 2017 年还只是学生们的玩笑。但到了 2025 年，当他突然从全职工作中被解雇时，他想起了这个项目，当时预测市场正在起飞。“我是一个有精神、有宗教信仰的人，”他说。“更世俗的人会说，这个机会是巧合。但在我看来，这在某种程度上是某种迹象。让我来谈谈这一点。”因此，Francia 启动了 Prediction Hunt，这是一个 Discord 频道和在线社区，成千上万的人聚集在一起，在 Polymarket 上交流如何赚钱并明智下注的技巧和想法。《卫报》在这个 Discord 频道上花费了大约三周的时间。有跟踪“淡出”投注的警报，您可以在其中尝试跟踪聪明的资金：例如，有利可图的钱包对伊朗政权将于 4 月 30 日垮台押注“是”，而无利可图的钱包则在押注“不”。有跟踪潜在内部人士的警报，因此您可以复制他们的赌注：其中之一似乎对美联储的利率决定有内线。妇女们举着已故伊朗最高领导人阿里·哈梅内伊和他的儿子莫伊塔巴·哈梅内伊的肖像，对 Mojtaba 是否会接替他的父亲进行赌注。摄影：Majid Saeedi/Getty Images 还有套利警报，您可以通过以下方式保证自己获利。 3 月初，这样的警报指出了 Polymarket 和另一个在线预测市场 Kalshi 之间关于 Mojtaba Khamenei 是否会成为伊朗下一任最高领导人的两组不同赔率。其运作方式如下：在 Polymarket 上以 33.6 美分的价格购买“是”，在竞争对手平台 Kalshi 上以 47 美分的价格购买“否”，这两个赌注将花费 80.6 美分，但其中一个是其中之一。他们肯定会赢——无论是莫杰塔巴·哈梅内伊（Mojtaba Khamenei）会被任命为继任者，还是不会被任命为继任者，正确的赌注的赔付是 1 美元，这意味着 19.4 美分的差额就是利润，投入 1,000 美元，您将获得 194 美元的利润。但与此同时，我听到人们说——比如，如果你在乌克兰，或者在某个正在经历战争和破坏的国家，你正处于战争迷雾中，对吧？“你不知道什么是宣传，什么是真实的，什么是假的。你的政府说一切都是安全的，是因为他们真的这么认为吗？还是因为他们想给人民灌输信心？这些都是合理的问题，Polymarket 是一个值得求助的好地方。”该频道的另一位用户迈克·凯恩 (Mike Kane) 表示，“框架很艰难”。 “我不知道是 Kalshi 还是 Polymarket——他们打赌，你知道，军队什么时候会驻扎在伊朗……我的意思是，这些都是即将发生的现实世界事件。它确实对我们的生活产生了影响。”Polymarket 用户可能会打赌唐纳德·特朗普在演讲中会说多少次“舞厅”这个词。照片：Aaron Schwartz/EPA 《卫报》采访了 Discord 频道的其他几位用户。一名美国高中生表示，他主要玩的是“提及市场”，用户押注某个公众人物在一次演讲中可能会说某个特定短语的次数，例如特朗普是否会说“舞厅”这个词。在这方面很容易获得优势；该学生在 YouTube 上研究了特朗普和其他人过去的演讲记录。他说，到目前为止，他已经赚了 200 美元，尽管他在其他地方的体育博彩上赔了钱。 另一位名为“Hacker666”的人表示，他主要在体育博彩上下注，并拒绝透露更多细节：“出于隐私方面的考虑，我已经因为我的财富而受到了多次威胁（原文如此）。” 2024 年 7 月，美国民意调查专家 Nate Silver 加入了 Polymarket 的顾问委员会。 Silver 因对 2012 年美国大选各州结果近乎完美的预测而闻名，他对这个想法感到兴奋：他称赞 Polymarket 及其“在帮助人们理解和规划未来方面发挥的日益重要的作用”。那一年，Polymarket 在 Silver 的民意调查平均值失败的地方取得了成功，准确地预测了特朗普在 2024 年美国总统大选中获胜。Silver 表示 Polymarket 将一切货币化的能力可能是积极的，让民意调查者、他说，它为那些“难以量化”的问题提供了“实时数据源”。民意调查专家兼作家内特·西尔弗表示，Polymarket“在帮助人们理解和规划未来方面发挥着越来越重要的作用”。 Photograph: Sarah M LeeMarkets are buying this. 10 月，纽约证券交易所的母公司洲际交易所表示，将向 Polymarket 投资高达 20 亿美元，并将很快开始分销Polymarket 对投资者的情绪分析。高盛在时事通讯中引用了Polymarket对美伊冲突的赔率；纳斯达克最近要求美国证券交易委员会批准上市与其指数挂钩的二元期权（是或否，类似于 Polymarket 的赌注）。支持者说这是最好的。民意调查失败；主流媒体缺乏叙事。福布斯专栏作家写道，预测市场是“比民意调查、专家或官方报告变化更快的‘真相信号’”。 “过去，打电话的人都是新闻频道，”凯恩说。 “过去，他们是重大事件的最终决定者。像这样的事情正式发生是因为 CNN 和福克斯新闻这么说。但多亏了 Polymarket，出现了一个新信号。”但如果 Polymarket 能够推动更大的市场，这就会引发黑暗的可能性。首先是 Polymarket 上的赌徒有可能操纵更大的市场。该平台上的许多彩池只有少数投注者，这意味着少量的现金可能会改变 Polymarket 为特定赛事提供的赔率。哥伦比亚大学市场设计教授亚什·卡诺里亚 (Yash Kanoria) 表示，该平台的数据共享“为通过扭曲 Polymarket 的赔率来操纵金融市场提供了机会”。较大的市场可能会追逐他们认为的内幕知识或“真相信号”——例如，美联储不会改变利率。接下来的问题是，当事件的结果受到质疑时，谁来决定 Polymarket 的“真相”。 周一，匿名用户“Harshad”在 Discord 频道中询问，他是否仍有“机会”赢得有关美军是否会在 4 月底进入伊朗的赌注。他的钱是“不”。但在美国周末采取行动营救一名在伊斯法罕上空执行任务时被击落的机组人员后，Polymarket 似乎正在将市场解析为“是”。一位用户写道：“卖吧，兄弟。”哈沙德写道：“我仍然认为美国进入伊朗意味着像委内瑞拉那样的正式地面入侵。”另一位用户表示，“你已经入侵了市场。”他指的是美国是否会夺取对伊朗部分地区控制权的另一项赌注。另一个人说：“请阅读规则。”一架 F-15E 攻击鹰。 Polymarket 用户争论一名在伊朗被击落的机组人员是否符合美军进入该国的资格。照片：Ben Margot/AP 目前，当出现争议时，Polymarket 上的市场由一群持有名为 UMA 的加密代币的匿名人士来解决。这是一种不同寻常的方式来决定所发生的事情。一些长期用户认为这会导致平台腐败。不同的人持有不同数量的 UMA，因此拥有不同的投票权。 目前尚不清楚谁是最大的 UMA 持有者，也不知道什么可能会影响他们的投票方式。最终押注UMA的人完全有可能是下了大笔的钱。 Cointelegraph 前研究员 Ben Yorke 表示：“按照协议，你应该以正确的方式投票。” “但是 [UMA] 总是出错。而且在某些情况下，投票是由一两个非常大的 UMA 持有者决定的。” UMA 持有者正在对世界事件做出自己的裁决。 Polymarket 上有数十个有争议的市场，一些赌徒对实际发生的事情与其他赌徒存在分歧 - 例如，拦截的导弹是否算作一次打击？特朗普在社交媒体上发布的帖子算不算正式停火声明？例如，持有者最近决定美军是否真的“在 4 月 30 日之前进入伊朗”。他们决定本周是否可以说美国和伊朗确实达成了停火。周一，以色列确认击毙了据称是哈马斯炸药专家的穆罕默德·达瓦德，从而于 4 月 6 日对加沙采取了“军事行动”，之后他们考虑了是否支付超过 60 万美元。目前，他们的投票很大程度上只影响 Harshad 和“neverLose”等匿名用户是否兑现。但如果 Polymarket 更像是一个“真相信号”，那么可能决定真相的人就是一群匿名者，他们选择拥有大量的加密货币代币——并且可能会在结果上有钱。如果这看起来很可怕——并且充满腐败——一些人会问这是否比管理更传统市场的规则和不透明度更糟糕。 “感觉就像是整个市场，对吧？看看南希·佩洛西和她的股票投资组合表现优于一切，真的很难说内幕交易在哪里停止，”约克说。 “这至少让预测市场更加清晰，因为至少你可以追踪钱包。对于标准普尔 500 指数，没有人真正知道谁在购买股票。” 洲际交易所和 Polymarket 没有回应置评请求。

Original Summary

  1. “Horekunden” was rapidly losing patience.His frustration was with the Institute for the Study of War, a US thinktank which produces a daily map of the frontline in Ukraine.For Horekunden, and other anonymous gamblers, the map was a “disjointed, incoherent mess … like the painting of a five-year-old”. Therefore it was no use to them in their aim: to settle a bet on the online prediction market Polymarket.The map they were unhappy with depicted the city of Kostyantynivka, which Ukrainian troops have been holding for five months amid shelling and swarms of drones. Thousands of civilians still live there.There is now more than $500,000 (£371,000) staked on whether Russia will capture Kostyantynivka this year . These bets will be settled if the ISW releases a map showing Russia holds the city’s train station.In a thriving online economy, thousands of people are discussing how to profit from war. They are betting on Ukraine. They were betting on the US-Iran ceasefire – $280m in play. They are betting on whether the US will invade Iran – $7.5m wagered.A Ukrainian serviceman walks in the rubble of residential buildings in Kostyantynivka, Ukraine. Polymarket users have bet on whether Russia will take the city. Photograph: Oleg Petrasiuk/24th Mechanized Brigade of Ukrainian Armed Forces/AFP/Getty ImagesIn groups hosted by the messaging app Discord, they debate what might come to pass. “Shit can go really wrong. WW3 may happen,” wrote a user this week who had money on whether the US would strike the oil terminal on Iran’s Kharg Island.“As long as America gets Iran’s uranium I can see all of the above peacefulness materializing,” wrote another, who appeared to be gambling on this week’s ceasefire.And, sometimes, they appear to be collaborating to influence the course of events, or at least how events are recorded. Critics have called this immoral; US senators have called for Polymarket to be regulated.Polymarket, however, views itself as a source of truth.Several weeks ago, amid reports that it appeared to be hosting insider bets on US strikes against Iran, it posted a “Note on Middle East Markets”.“After discussing with those directly affected by the attacks, who had dozens of questions, we realized that prediction markets could give them the answers they needed in ways TV news and X could not,” it said.The rise and rise of PolymarketIn July 2024, shortly before Donald Trump was re-elected in the US, Polymarket had reported about $400m total traded on its platforms that year. It now can trade upward of that in a single day. It calls itself a “prediction market” – a way to gather data on the future by offering the public a way to bet on it.But longtime users say it increasingly resembles a casino – where everything, from Trump’s fury to the second coming of Jesus Christ can be monetised. So where they can, players in this powerful “casino” have started trying to shape the world to secure a payday.In July 2024, about $400m had been traded on Polymarket’s platforms. Photograph: Abaca Press/AlamyPolymarket gamblers recently threatened an Israeli journalist, demanding he change his copy so that they could win a bet over whether Iran had struck Israel on a given day. Experts say that users on Polymarket might be able to manipulate broader markets, with effects that could ripple out to institutions and pension funds.As for the Ukrainian frontline, after venting about ISW’s map on the Discord channel, Horekunden decided to take things further. He appealed to another user, @tsybka, whose X profile describes him as a “top 0.001% trader” on the platform.“You managed to contact them once before with your outreach, so I’d appreciate it if you could try passing this along,” Horekunden wrote.“This is nonsense, they won’t listen to anyone,” replied @tsybka, who did not respond to an inquiry from the Guardian.“Either way this map is so fucking bad it’s incredible,” he wrote. Then he decided to telephone ISW himself. “Unfortunately there’s nothing we can do because all the other mappers are too easy to bribe.”The ISW insisted it did “not engage with the users or representatives of such platforms”.“We strongly condemn and have never consented to the exploitation of our products for the abhorrent purpose of gambling on war,” said a spokesperson.‘No one wants to make money off war or people dying, but …’“I love to gamble,” said Joseph Francia.Now in his early 30s, Francia counted cards in casinos while studying economics at Berkeley, and spent weekends in Reno, Nevada, playing blackjack. He’s not a thrill-seeking “Yolo” (you only live once) gambler, he said: he likes to bet when he has an edge on the house.At university, he and a friend decided to collect data from a number of offshore sportsbooks, and start placing arbitrage bets: playing on the discrepancies in odds given by different betting sites.“If the odds on the Lakers are really good on one site, and the odds on the Pacers are really good on another site, you could bet on basically both teams on different sportsbooks and make guaranteed profit,” he said.That project was a student lark in 2017. But in 2025, he remembered it when he was suddenly laid off from his full-time job, just as prediction markets were taking off.“I’m a spiritual, religious person,” he said. “The more secular people would say, this opportunity is coincidence. But in my head, I was like, this is a sign of something to some extent. Let me lean into this.”So Francia started Prediction Hunt, a Discord channel and online community where thousands of people gather to trade tips and ideas for how to make money – and bet smart – on Polymarket. The Guardian spent roughly three weeks in this Discord channel.There are alerts to track “fade” bets, where you try to follow the smart money: profitable wallets were betting “yes” on the Iranian regime falling by 30 April, for example, while unprofitable wallets were betting “no”.There are alerts to track potential insiders, so you can copy their bets: one of these appears to have an inside line on interest rate decisions by the US Federal Reserve.Women hold portraits of the late supreme leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei and his son Mojtaba Khamenei. Polymarket hosted bets on whether Mojtaba would take over from his father. Photograph: Majid Saeedi/Getty ImagesAnd there are the arbitrage alerts, where you can guarantee yourself a profit by playing odds across multiple markets. In early March, one such alert pointed at two different sets of odds, between Polymarket and another online prediction market, Kalshi, on whether Mojtaba Khamenei would be the next supreme leader of Iran.Here is how that would work: buy “yes” on Polymarket for 33.6 cents and “no” on Kalshi, a rival platform, for 47 cents. You would spend 80.6 cents for these two bets, but one of them is guaranteed to win – either Mojtaba Khamenei would or would not be named successor. The payout for a correct bet is $1, meaning the 19.4 cents difference is profit. Put down $1,000 and you would make $194 in profit.“Those are really tricky because I think there absolutely is a gruesome aspect to it,” said Francia, asked about the bets on war.“No one wants to make money off war or some amount of people dying. But at the same time, I’ve heard people say – like, if you’re in Ukraine, or in some country going through war and devastation, and you’re in the fog of war, right?“You don’t know what’s propaganda, what’s true, what’s fake. Is your government saying everything’s safe because they actually believe so? Or because they want to instil confidence in their people? Those are legitimate questions and a good place to turn to is Polymarket.”War betting is “tough frame”, said Mike Kane, another user of the channel. “I don’t know if it was Kalshi or Polymarket – they had a bet on, you know, when will troops be on the ground in Iran … I mean, those are real-world events that are going to happen. And it does have an impact on our lives.”Polymarket users might bet on how many times Donald Trump might say the word ‘ballroom’ in a speech. Photograph: Aaron Schwartz/EPAThe Guardian spoke to several other users of the Discord channel. A US high school student said he mostly played in the “mentions markets”, where users bet on how many times a public figure might say a specific phrase in a speech – for example, whether or not Trump might say the word “ballroom”.It was easy to get an edge in this; the student studied transcripts of past speeches, by Trump and others, on YouTube. So far he’d made $200, he said, although he’d elsewhere lost money on sports bets.Another named “Hacker666” said he mostly bet on sports, and declined to give further detail: “I dont give out personal information … Due to privacy concerns as i have received multiple threaths (sic) already because of my wealth.”Anonymous group that rules on the truthIn July 2024, the US pollster Nate Silver joined the advisory board of Polymarket. Silver, known for his near-perfect prediction of the state-by-state outcome of the 2012 US election, was excited about this idea: he praised Polymarket and its “increasingly vital role in helping people understand and plan for the future”.That year, Polymarket succeeded where Silver’s polling averages failed, accurately predicting Trump’s win in the 2024 US presidential elections.Silver suggested Polymarket’s ability to monetise everything could be positive, allowing pollsters, statisticians and the interested public to study, in far greater depth, the hurly-burly of events.It offered a “real-time data source” about questions that were otherwise “hard to quantify”, he said.The pollster and author Nate Silver said Polymarket played an ‘increasingly vital role in helping people understand and plan for the future’. Photograph: Sarah M LeeMarkets are buying this. In October, the Intercontinental Exchange, the owner of the New York Stock Exchange, said it would invest up to $2bn into Polymarket – and would soon start distributing Polymarket’s sentiment analysis to investors. Goldman Sachs has cited Polymarket’s odds on the US-Iran conflict in newsletters; the Nasdaq has recently asked the SEC to approve listing binary options – yes-no Polymarket-like bets – tied to its index. Boosters say this is for the best. Polls are failing; mainstream media is missing the narrative. Prediction markets are “a ‘truth signal’ that moves faster than polls, pundits, or official reports”, wrote a Forbes columnist. “It used to be the news channels were the callers,” said Kane. “They used to be the final say in big events. Like this officially happened because CNN and Fox News said so. But thanks to Polymarket, there’s a new signal.” But if Polymarket can move larger markets, this raises dark eventualities. First is the possibility that gamblers on Polymarket could manipulate far larger markets. Many pools on the platform have only a few bettors, meaning small amounts of well-laid cash could change what Polymarket gives as the odds for a certain event. The platform’s data sharing “opens up an opportunity to manipulate financial markets by skewing the odds on Polymarket”, said Yash Kanoria, a professor at Columbia who works on market design. Larger markets might chase what they believe to be insider knowledge, or a “truth signal” – for example, that the Federal Reserve won’t change interest rates. Then there’s the issue of who decides Polymarket’s “truth” when the outcome of an event is in question.On Monday, anonymous user “Harshad” asked in a Discord channel if there was “any chance” that he could still win his bet about whether US forces would enter Iran by the end of April. His money was on “no”.But Polymarket appeared to be resolving the market to “yes”, after the US conducted an operation to rescue a crew member shot down on a mission over Isfahan over the weekend.“Just sell bro,” wrote one user.“I still think usa enter in Iran means official ground invasion like venezuela,” wrote Harshad.“You have invade market for that,” said another user, referring to a separate bet on whether the US will seize control over a part of Iran’s territory.“Read the rules,” said another.An F-15E Strike Eagle. Polymarket users debated whether a crew member shot down over Iran qualified as US forces entering the country. Photograph: Ben Margot/APAt the moment, when there is a dispute, markets on Polymarket are settled by an anonymous group of people who hold a crypto token called UMA.It’s an unusual way to decide what has happened. Some longtime users suggest it opens the platform to corruption. Different individuals hold different amounts of UMA, and therefore have different voting power.It isn’t known who the largest UMA holders are, or what might affect how they vote. It is entirely possible that the people who finally settle a bet on UMA have large amounts of money staked on it. “It’s the protocol that you should vote the right way,” said Ben Yorke, formerly a researcher at Cointelegraph. “But [UMA] gets it wrong all the time. And there have been cases where votes are decided by, like, one or two very large UMA holders.”UMA holders are coming to their own rulings about world events. There are tens of disputed markets on Polymarket, where some gamblers disagree with others about what really took place – for example, does an intercepted missile count as a strike? Does a social media post from Trump count as a formal declaration of a ceasefire?The holders recently decided, for example, if US forces really did “enter Iran by 30 April”. They decided whether it can be said that the US and Iran really did reach a ceasefire this week. And they reviewed whether to pay out over $600,000 after Israel confirmed that it killed Muhammad Dawad, allegedly a Hamas explosives expert, on Monday, thus taking a “military action against Gaza” on 6 April.Right now, their votes largely just affect whether anonymous users such as Harshad and “neverLose” cash out.But if Polymarket becomes more of a “truth signal”, then the people who might decide what that truth is are a group of anonymous people who have chosen to own large amounts of a cryptocurrency token – and may have money on an outcome.If this seems frightening – and fraught with corruption – some ask if it is any worse than the rules, and opacities, that govern more traditional markets. “It just feels like it’s the whole market, right? Look at, like, Nancy Pelosi and her stock portfolios outperforming everything, it’s really hard to say where the insider trading stops,” said Yorke. “That sort of makes prediction markets at least a little bit more clear, because at least you can track the wallets. With the S&P 500, no one knows, really, who’s buying stocks.”The Intercontinental Exchange and Polymarket did not respond to requests for comment.

nunez 2026/04/11 22:33
不是新现象！这发生在伊拉克战争期间。 https://www.nytimes.com/2003/03/02/weekinreview/you-can-bet-...

人们绝对会在任何事情上下注；赌博与时间一样古老。

> 根据英国普通法，赌博通常是合法的，只要它不 导致不朽或不礼貌。13 对战争事件结果的赌注 政治领导人因法庭案件或选民之间因选举结果而死亡的事件 14 在维多利亚时代和爱德华时代，英国 政府越来越多地试图限制赌博，特别是在工薪阶层中 类。 1845 年的《博彩法》使得赌博合同和债务在以下国家无法执行： 法庭（但以其他方式放宽了赌注金额）；博彩公司法 1853 年颁布的法令禁止私人俱乐部以外的博彩机构的经营；的 1874 年《博彩公司法》严厉打击博彩广告；和 1906 年街头投注法允许在街头和公共场所进行投注 15 尽管 19 世纪末和 20 世纪初法律存在不确定性，舰队 街头媒体报道了伦敦证券交易所和劳合社的选举投注 议会“多数派”的市场。 [^0]

[^0] Rhode, Paul W.“政治博彩市场的悠久历史。” KU 商学院，2012 年 3 月。 https://users.wfu.edu/strumpks/papers/Int_Election_Betting_F...

Not a new phenomenon! It happened during the War on Iraq. https://www.nytimes.com/2003/03/02/weekinreview/you-can-bet-...

People will bet on absolutely anything; gambling is as old as time itself.

> Wagering was generally legal under British common law, so long as it did not to lead to immortality or impolity.13 Bets about the outcome of events in war, over the death of political leaders, over court cases, or between voters over election results were illegal on these grounds.14 In the Victorian and Edwardian periods, the British government increasingly attempted to limit gambling, especially among the working classes. The Gaming Act of 1845 made gambling contracts and debts unenforceable in court (but otherwise liberalized what amounts could be wagered); the Betting Houses Act of 1853 outlawed the operation of betting establishments other than private clubs; the Betting Houses Act of 1874 cracked down of the advertisement of wagering; and the Street Betting Act of 1906 made acceptance of wagers in streets and public places illegal.15 Despite the legal uncertainty in the late 19th and early 20th century, the Fleet Street press reported on election wagering at the London Stock Exchange and at Lloyd’s in markets for Parliamentary “majorities.” [^0]

[^0] Rhode, Paul W. “The Long History of Political Betting Markets.” KU School of Business, 2012 March. https://users.wfu.edu/strumpks/papers/Int_Election_Betting_F...

您的评论对对话起到了很好的补充作用。不过，我只想添加比例点。该来源押注不到一百万，现在有数亿人押注于停火，还有数百万押于其他伊朗，然后更多押注于俄罗斯/乌克兰。

所以我认为将其描述为有点夸大其词，因为没有什么新鲜事。但你的历史背景很有帮助。

Your comment is a great addition to the conversation. I do just want to add the scale point, though. That source is less than a million was bet, and now there are hundreds of millions bet on the ceasefire, and millions more on other Iran bets, and then even more bet on Russia/Ukraine.

So I think it's a bit overstated to frame it as nothing is new. But your historical context is helpful.

Wagering was commonly legal, but generally illegal since 1541 Henry VIII banned for laborers & servants.

PaulRobinson 2026/04/12 00:41
军火公司的每个股东（或指数跟踪器的持有者）也都在押注战争。

这种机制是愚蠢的、腐败的，并且会导致极端行为。

但押注战争并不新鲜，我觉得令人憎恶的是，人们放弃了作为现代文明重要组成部分的军工综合体，同时给押注战争的个人起了可怕的名字。

在我看来，这只是规模和语义，而不是核心差异。

Every shareholder of an arms company (or holder of an index tracker), is betting on war too.

This mechanism is stupid, corrupt and driving extreme behaviour.

But betting on war is not new, and I find it abhorrent that people will hand wave away the military-industrial complex as an essential component to modern civilisation while calling individuals making bets on war horrible names.

It's just scale and semantics, not core differences to my eye.

在 HN 中查看
echelon 2026/04/12 00:15
它还将直接或间接地导致暗杀市场。

> “约翰尼首席执行​​官将于 2027 年在世”

这可能会被平台禁止（尽管不是非法的），但这不是：

> “Jonny 首席执行官是 ComanyAbc 股票过热的主要原因。CompanyAbc 股票将于 2027 年 12 月 31 日收盘价高于 X 市值。”

这是暗杀的间接代理。

这里可能发生多种情况：

- 刺客对暗杀有利的结果下注，然后暗杀。

- 对手对不利于暗杀的结果下注，以声称缺乏责任或吸引不良结果。他们可能会公开宣布，然后悄悄地实施结果，或者让它自然出现，以接受赌注的另一方。

我可以想到许多首席执行官、政治家和世界领导人，这将使他们成为众矢之的，并使市场参与者变得非常富有。

即使是“X 候选人赢得 Y 选举”的结果也受此影响。

It's also going to lead to assassination markets, either directly or indirectly.

> "Johnny CEO will be alive in 2027"

That might be banned from platforms (though not illegal), but this isn't:

> "Jonny CEO is the primary reason ComanyAbc stock is so overheated. CompanyAbc stock will close above X market cap on December 31, 2027."

That is an indirect proxy for assassination.

There are multiple things that could happen here:

- Assassin(s) place bets for outcome favored by assassination, then assassinate.

- Rival(s) place bets for outcome that do not favor assassination to either claim lack of responsibility or to attract bad outcomes. They might publicly announce it, then quietly work on implementing the outcome or let it arise naturally to take the other side of the bet.

I can think of a number of CEOs, politicians, and world leaders where this would put them in the crosshairs and make market participants very wealthy.

Even the "X candidate wins Y election" outcomes are subject to this.

ffsm8 2026/04/11 22:39
我对这个论点的理解是这样的：如果有人打赌战争不会开始，而你能够发动战争……那么每个押注战争不会开始的人实际上都是在为你提供风险投资资金来发动那场战争。

所以如果例如。 2000万打赌它不会开始，持有众所周知的扳机的演员只需要“投资”足够的资金来耗尽赌注，然后通过扣动扳机来利用它，每个反对它的人都会有效地投入到战争中

这个类比在风险投资中不成立，因为他们期望在成功融资后获得回报，而这并不能提供。

我认为这更像是 Kickstarter/战争众筹。就跟操蛋一样

（我在这里使用的“发动战争”设置只是为了说明这一点。还有很多更细粒度的赌注正在进行，例如（地点）将受到争夺等，从而有效地为进攻创造了资金池）

I understood the argument like this: If there is a bet going that a war doesn't start, and you're able to start said war... Then everyone betting on the war not starting is effectively providing venture capital funds for you to start that war.

So if eg. 20 mil is bet on it not starting, the actor holding the proverbial trigger only needs to "invest" sufficient funds to drain the bet and then capitalize on it by pulling the trigger, everyone being against it would've effectively invested into the war

The analogy breaks apart at VC, because they're expecting payout after successful funding, which this doesn't provide.

I think it's more like Kickstarter/crowdfunding for wars. Just as fucked up though

(The "start a war" setting I'm using here is just to illustrate the point. There are a lot more granular bets going on like (place) will be contested etc, effectively creating money pools for offensives)

在 HN 中查看
azan_ 2026/04/12 01:35
Polymarket 没有足够的流动性来实现这一点。然而，股票市场上有足够的流动性，而且实际上似乎在战争开始和停火期间存在大量内幕交易。当涉及到也可以在股票市场上实现的赌注时，Polymarket 确实是小菜一碟。
There's not enough liquidity on polymarket for that. However there's enough liquidity on stock market and indeed it appears that there was a lot of insider trading around start of the war and ceasefire. Polymarket is really pennies when it comes to bets that can be also realized on the stock market.

PowerElectronix 2026/04/11 21:15

Now, can you find enough liquidity in the market to turn a profit? Can you find it before it becomes aparent something is off in the bet?
在 HN 中查看
daveguy 2026/04/11 22:37
这是一场 0-1 的赌注，无论以哪种方式解决。如果您能够下注，那么流动性就存在。但是，是的，如果你不能在不显着推动市场的情况下首先下注，那么你就不会赚那么多。

预测市场不应该合法。

It's a 0-1 bet that resolves one way or the other. If you were able to place the bet, the liquidity is there. But, yeah, if you can't make the bet in the first place without significantly pushing the market then you won't make as much.

Prediction markets should not be legal.

在 HN 中查看
aleksiy123 2026/04/12 05:10
我觉得有两种方法可以构建这个框架。

第一批人对他们想要的结果与他们认为会发生的结果进行投注。

我认为第二个显然是有道理的。如果有两位专家争论战争将如何发展，并且他们愿意在这场战争上下注，那是件好事，因为他们“言出必行”或“利益攸关”。这是一件好事。

第一个意味着操纵或只是一个道德问题。

我想问题是不可能两者缺一不可。但我猜大多数人下注是因为第二个原因而不是第一个。

I feel like there is two ways to frame this.

The first people betting on the outcome they want vs outcome they think will happen.

I think the second obviously make sense. If you had two experts arguing over how a war would go and they where willing to make a bet on it that is good because they are putting “their money where their mouth is” or have “skin in the game”. That’s a good thing.

The first implies manipulation or just a moralistic problem.

The issue I guess is it’s not possible to have one without the other. But I would guess most people are betting because of the second reason and not the first.

在 HN 中查看
daft_pink 2026/04/12 04:12
只是想重申，预测市场是有益的，因为它们是为我们提供预测复杂系统未来结果的动态机制的最准确方法。

正如 James Suroweicki 在《群体的智慧》一书中提出的那样，预测市场提供的预测往往比任何专家、专家团队等都更准确。这并不意味着它 100% 准确，但它往往比我们拥有的任何其他机制都更准确。

好处不是赌徒有地方赌博，而是社会有办法衡量不同结果的风险并做出相应的计划。

Just want to reiterate that prediction markets are beneficial, because they are the most accurate way to provide us with a dynamic mechanism to predict future outcomes from complicated systems.

As was put forth in James Suroweicki’s the Wisdom of Crowds, prediction markets provide predictions that tend to be more accurate than any expert, team of experts, etc. That doesn’t mean it’s 100% accurate, but it tends to be more accurate than any other mechanism that we have.

The benefit is not that gamblers have a place to gamble, but that society has a way to gauge the risks of different outcomes and plan accordingly.

在 HN 中查看
Peritract 2026/04/12 04:28
我不确定“有好处”是否直接映射到“有好处”。你还必须考虑不利的一面，例如有影响力的人故意导致X事件发生（例如战争），以便他们能够获利。

我不确定增加可预测性是否值得增加不稳定性。

I'm not sure 'have a benefit' maps directly to 'are beneficial'. You also have to consider the downsides, such as people with influence deliberately causing X event to happen (e.g. a war) so that they can profit.

I'm not sure the increased predictability is worth the increased instability.

在 HN 中查看
scoofy 2026/04/12 09:33
>只是想重申一下，预测市场是有益的，因为它们是为我们提供预测复杂系统未来结果的动态机制的最准确方法。

抱歉，不，这绝对是 错误的。

如果每个人都真诚地做出这些预测，那么它确实会“为我们提供一个动态机制来预测复杂系统的未来结果”。然而，当涉及到大量资金时，内部人士实际上想要 市场权重是否不正确 在织女星和西塔最小化的那一刻最大化增益......在最后一秒可能。

市场反身性 存在 并破坏了整个论文。人们需要停止假装每个人都是像内特·西弗（Nate Siver）这样的好人，这样做只是为了好玩。只要内部人士存在并被允许进行交易，那么他们就有充分的动机诱导错误信息，直到他们可以进行相反交易的最后一刻。

>Just want to reiterate that prediction markets are beneficial, because they are the most accurate way to provide us with a dynamic mechanism to predict future outcomes from complicated systems.

I'm sorry, but no, this is just absolutely wrong.

If everyone was making these predictions, in good-faith, then yes it would "provide us with a dynamic mechanism to predict future outcomes from complicated systems." However, when truckloads of money is involved, then insiders actually want to have the markets be weighted incorrectly to maximize gain at exactly the point where vega and theta are minimized... at the very last second possible.

Market reflexivity exists and undermines this entire thesis. People need to stop pretending everyone out there is a nice man like Nate Siver just doing this for fun. As long as insiders exist and are allowed to trade, then they have every incentive to induce misinformation until the very last moment when they can trade against it.

arter45 2026/04/11 22:16
所以...

>“过去，来电者都是新闻频道，”凯恩说。 “过去，他们是重大事件的最终决定者。像这样的事情正式发生是因为 CNN 和福克斯新闻这么说。但多亏了 Polymarket，出现了一个新信号。”

这很有趣，因为：

> 目前，当出现争议时，Polymarket 上的市场由一群持有名为 UMA 的加密代币的匿名人士来解决。

[...]

> 目前尚不清楚谁是最大的 UMA 持有者，也不知道什么可能会影响他们的投票方式。最终押注UMA的人完全有可能是下了大笔的钱。

因此，基本上，你不相信 CNN 和福克斯新闻，而是相信一群匿名的人，他们可能会也可能不会从自己的决定中获利。我不明白这有什么更好的地方。

So...

>“It used to be the news channels were the callers,” said Kane. “They used to be the final say in big events. Like this officially happened because CNN and Fox News said so. But thanks to Polymarket, there’s a new signal.”

This is interesting because of this:

>At the moment, when there is a dispute, markets on Polymarket are settled by an anonymous group of people who hold a crypto token called UMA.

[...]

>It isn’t known who the largest UMA holders are, or what might affect how they vote. It is entirely possible that the people who finally settle a bet on UMA have large amounts of money staked on it.

So basically instead of trusting CNN and Fox News you trust an anonymous group of people who may or may not profit themselves from their own decisions. I can't see how this is any better.

在 HN 中查看
zarzavat 2026/04/11 22:29
不。这些解决程序仅处理规则争议，即有关细则的争议。这仅适用于您在 Polymarket 赌博的情况。市场开放时的概率由市场力量决定。

当然，您必须非常仔细地阅读细则才能了解特定市场的含义。

更具欺骗性的是标题和规则之间潜在的差异。大多数市场参与者都会仔细阅读规则（尽管有些人没有），因此标题+标题概率可能并不像看上去的那样。

No. These resolvers only deal with rules disputes, i.e. disputes over the fine print. This is only applicable if you are gambling on polymarket. The probabilities while the market is open are set by market forces.

Of course you have to read the fine print very carefully to understand what a particular market is about.

The more deceptive thing is the potential disparity between the title and the rules. Most market participants read the rules closely (although some do not), and so the title + headline probabilities may be not as they seem.

在 HN 中查看
arter45 2026/04/11 22:57
是的，但例如，如果我看一下当前对“针对伊朗的军事行动结束于……”的押注，它谈论的是美国和以色列都没有对伊朗领土发起无人机导弹或空袭的日期。这看起来相当可靠，但我们知道两国甚至在当前冲突发生之前就已经发动了无人机或导弹袭击。

因此，如果战争结束，但美国派出一架无人机专门杀死一个人，甚至可能是一名与政府无关的恐怖分子，这算不算？细则并不总是那么详细，尤其是对于某些类型的事件。

这对于博彩平台来说并不奇怪，但如果这个博彩平台将自己定位为一种众筹新闻媒体，那么统治细则本质上就意味着统治新闻。显然，所有新闻媒体都决定他们应该发布哪些新闻，但至少对于传统媒体，你往往会了解他们的偏见、控制他们的人等等。相反，细则，以及最终（在 Polymarket 看来）世界新闻真相的来源，依赖于一群匿名人士投票选出他们认为是世界事件的实际意义的内容。

Yes but for example if I look at the current bet on “Military action against Iran ends on…” it talks about the date on which neither US nor Israel initiate a drone missile or air strike on Iranian soil. This seems pretty solid, but we know both countries have launched drone or missile strike even before the current conflict.

So if the war ends but then the US sends a drone to kill one guy in particular, maybe even a terrorist not affiliated with the government, does that count? The fine print is not always that detailed especially for some kinds of events.

This is not surprising for a betting platform, but if this betting platform positions itself as a sort of crowdfunded news outlet, ruling over the fine print essentially means ruling over the news. Obviously all news outlets decide which news they should publish, but at least with traditional outlets you tend to know their biases, the group of people controlling them and so on. Instead the fine print, and ultimately (in Polymarket’s view) the source of the truth about world news, relies on a group of anonymous people voting for what they believe to be the actual meaning of world events.

_alternator_ 2026/04/11 22:41
我认为 GP 的意思是，原则上 UMA 代币持有者可以以他们认为合适的任何方式解决争议，即使实际上是根据规则解决的。归根结底，必须有人为了支付目的而仲裁“真相”。
I think GP is saying that, in principle, the UMA token holders can resolve a dispute any way they see fit, even if in practice it's resolved according to the rules. At the end of the day there is someone who has to arbitrate 'truth' for the purposes of payout.
在 HN 中查看
zarzavat 2026/04/11 23:55
我明白这一点。我想说的是，决心只与投注者相关。它不会影响任何使用 Polymarket 作为被动信息源的人，因为没有人根据市场的决议更新他们的世界观，他们根据价格行为更新他们的世界观。价格走势遵循预期决议，而不是实际决议。

即使有人以错误的方式博弈决策，让市场以错误的方式决策，也不会影响市场在决策之前对概率的定价。

I understand that. What I'm saying is that resolution is only relevant to bettors. It doesn't affect anyone using Polymarket as a passive information source, because nobody is updating their world view based on the resolution of a market, they are updating their world view based on the price action. The price action follows the expected resolution, not the actual resolution.

Even if someone were to game resolution to make a market resolve the wrong way, it wouldn't affect how the market priced the probabilities in the time before the resolution.

_alternator_ 2026/04/12 04:49

It would definitely affect the price if there were an expected bias in resolution.

janoelze 2026/04/11 22:56
这是一个不完美的系统，但 UMA 的决定也可能会再次受到争议，最多可达两次 iirc，这会将争议/决定转移到不同的 UMA 鲸鱼组。说实话，我认为这已经是最好的了——除非你完全置身事外。
it's an imperfect system, but UMA decisions can also be disputed again, up to two times iirc, which moves the dispute/decision to a different group of UMA whales. tbh, i think this is as good as it gets – unless you stay out of this completely.

“资本主义”：全人类不可避免地沦为可替代货币
'Capitalism': the inevitable reduction of all humanity to fungible currency
在 HN 中查看
jancsika 2026/04/11 22:27
这一定是真的吗？

例如，假设我正在网上购物并被放入行为直方图箱 #1 中。由于冲动的浏览习惯和电池电量不足等原因，您处于第 2 类垃圾箱中。因此，你的薯条箱价格比我的贵 x%。

现在，假设我们俩从同一地点分别乘坐优步。类似的数据箱最终会导致你的电池电量不足，导致 Uber 的价格上涨 y%。

在我看来，足够的整合和基于行为数据的定价实际上阻碍了货币的可替代性。因为虽然你和我仍然可以直接互相借入和偿还货币而不受惩罚，但我们用该货币购买的商品和服务的字面价格将会有所不同。也就是说，如果你周一给我买了一个三明治，而我周二还给你一个三明治，那么你就赔钱了。

编辑：对于我所说的钢铁侠，想象一下大多数杂货店和便利店已经从静态定价转向二维码之类的东西。此外，假设基于个人数据的定价在大多数基本私人商品和服务的各个行业中都很普遍。

Is that necessarily true?

E.g., suppose I'm grocery shopping online and get put in behavioral histogram bin #1. You're in bin #2 because of stuff like impulsive browsing habits and low battery. Your bin's price for chips is consequently x% more expensive than mine.

Now, suppose both of us get separate uber rides from the same location. Similar data bins end up with your low battery generating y% higher price for your Uber.

Seems to me enough consolidation and behavioral data-based pricing practically impedes the fungibility of currency. Because while you and I can still borrow and pay back the currency directly to each other with impunity, the literal price of goods and services we can buy with that currency will be different. I.e., if you buy me a sandwich on Monday and I pay you back with a sandwich on Tuesday, you're losing money.

Edit: for the steel man of what I'm saying, imagine most grocery and convenience stores have shifted away from static pricing to something like qr codes. Also, assume pricing based on personal data is rampant across industries for most basic private goods and services.

在 HN 中查看
miki123211 2026/04/11 23:05
事实是，价格差异化是我们一直在做的事情 世纪，只是有更糟糕的启发法。

当你用一个群体比其他群体支付更多的费用来描述它时，人们就会被触发。然而，如果有一个基本上没有人支付的标价，并且大多数客户根据启发法所说的富有程度获得折扣，那突然就没问题了。

在欧洲大部分地区，交通票都是这样使用的。有标价，但大多数人不支付标价。实际上，大多数门票是由小学生、大学生、老年人等购买的，并且都有不同程度的折扣。无论你认为它是“学生折扣”还是“可能有钱人附加费”，其实都没关系，最终的结果都是一样的。这同样适用于电影院、博物馆、游乐园。在这里，如果您有“大家庭卡”，您甚至可以享受一些杂货连锁店的折扣。

The truth is, price differentiation is something we've been doing for centuries, just with much worse heuristics.

People are triggered when you frame it in terms of one cohort paying more than the rest. However, if there's a sticker price that basically nobody pays, with most customers getting a discount based on how rich the heuristics say they are, that's suddenly fine.

Transit tickets work this way in most of Europe. There is a sticker price, but most people don't pay the sticker price. In practice, most tickets are purchased by school children, university students, seniors etc, and they all have varying levels of discounts. Whether you think of it as a "student discount" or as a "probably-rich-person surcharge", it doesn't really matter, in the end, the result is the same. Same applies to cinemas, museums, amusement parks. Here, you even have some grocery store chains that give you discounts if you have a "large family card."

在 HN 中查看
jancsika 2026/04/12 01:41
坚持钢铁侠。

如果我们的现金系统认为具有某些序列号的美元仅值 0.80 美元，因为它们有非法毒品交易的历史，那么该现金就不再是可替代的。

由于用户无法访问的数字档案的可靠推论，大多数卖家针对特定消费者的 1 美元必需品突然变成 1.20 美元，这在功能上有何不同？

在这两种情况下，消费者信心都会受到影响。我看到的最大的区别是，有一群狂热的人正确地喊道：“别他妈的搞砸了！”关于货币可替代性，还有一群人说，关于监视资本主义，“这很复杂”。

编辑：再次澄清一下——我说的是单独定制的价格，它会不知不觉地影响必需品整合行业中的大量消费者。

编辑2：我知道监视资本主义与使货币不可替代不是一回事。我正在寻找有关消费者信心和其他经济影响方面的差异的见解。

编辑3：澄清以缩小我的问题范围。如果你还看不出来，我正在认真地向学习经济学的人寻求知识。

Stick to the steel man.

If our cash system deems dollars with certain serial numbers worth only $0.80 because they have history in the illicit drug trade, that cash is no longer fungible.

How is that functionally different than a system where a dollar of essential goods suddenly becomes $1.20 across most sellers for a particular consumer due to reliable inferences from an digital dossier inaccessible to the user?

In both cases, consumer confidence suffers. The biggest difference I see is that there's a rabid contingent who correctly yell, "Don't fucking mess with that!" with regard to currency fungibility, and a bunch of people saying, "It's complicated," with regard to surveillance capitalism.

Edit: again, to be clear-- I'm talking about individually tailored prices insidiously affecting large numbers of consumers in a consolidated industry for essential goods.

Edit 2: I know surveillance capitalism isn't the same thing as making currency be non-fungible. I'm looking for insight on what the difference is in terms of consumer confidence and other economic impacts.

Edit 3: clarification to narrow my question. If you can't tell yet, I'm earnestly looking for knowledge from someone who studies economics.

NeutralCrane 2026/04/12 01:11
你这么说，好像没有多少人觉得 2500 美元不是一笔巨款。即使在美国这样的富裕国家，这也相当于普通人个人收入的约 7%。但对于世界上 65% 的每天生活费为 10 美元或以下的人口来说，他们的年收入增加了 77% 或更多。

但比现金更重要的是金钱可以购买的力量。揭露超级富豪对社会的不当影响将带来深远的好处，而不仅仅是人们银行账户里的钱。

You say that as if there aren’t an enormous amount of people for whom $2500 isn’t an enormous amount of money. Even in wealthy nations like the US, that equates to approximately 7% of the personal income for the average person. But for the 65% of the world’s population living on $10 or less per day, that is an increase of 77% or more on their yearly income.

But more important than the cash is the power that money buys. Defenestrating the uber-wealthy of their undue influence in society would have far reaching benefits beyond just money in people’s bank accounts.

ModernMech 2026/04/11 22:40
一旦资本家对政府有了足够的控制权，他们就会为自己操纵一切。在资本主义制度中，金钱至高无上，而他们拥有最多的金钱，因此他们的意见被认为高于其他人。一旦资本家为自己操纵这个系统，获得成功的唯一途径就是纯粹的运气或关系。由于不是每个人都可以连接，所以对于大多数人来说，运气是唯一的，因此它与嫖娼和战争一起成为经济活动的重要组成部分。另请参阅：OnlyFans 和战争部。
Once the capitalists get enough control of the government they rig everything for themselves. In a capitalist system money is paramount and they have the most of it, so their opinion is regarded higher than everyone else's. Once capitalists rig the system for themselves, the only way to get ahead is just pure luck or connections. Sine not everyone can be connected, luck is all that's left for most people, so it becomes a huge part of economic activity along with whoring and warring. 在 HN 中查看
nout 2026/04/12 00:48
我认为主要原因（实际上是相当反资本主义的）是银行和当权者有能力从无到有地创造金钱，将钱借给存在，同时贬低其他人的价值。

这对我来说非常有趣，我终于了解了银行（以及整个系统）如何创造货币。 作为一家银行，您从 0 美元开始，借给某人 100 美元，并将其“存入”他们在您银行的账户中。因此，现在您的账簿上有 100 的负债（该人在您帐户中的资金）和 100 的资产（该人欠您的钱）。所以会计是平衡的。你一开始并不需要钱，作为一家银行，你只需“借钱而存在”。

I think the main cause (that's actually quite anti-capitalist) is that banks and people at power have the ability to create money out of nothing, to lend the money into existence, while devaluing everyone else.

It was very interesting to me where I finally understood how banks (and the overall system) create money. As a bank you start with 0 money, you lend 100 to some person that you "deposit" into their account at your bank. So now on your books you have 100 in liabilities (the money that the person has in your account) and 100 in assets (the money the person owes you). So accounting is balanced. You did not need money to start with, as a bank you just "lend it into existence".

ModernMech 2026/04/11 22:33
看看你的周围，经济正在完全围绕赌博进行调整。从比特币到 nfts，到股票市场，到人工智能，到艺术，到约会应用程序，到社交媒体，到视频游戏，到风险投资，再到渗透到我们手机广告体育中的赌博应用程序。最后，我们有真正的政府成员在政策上赌博。

伴随你成长的资本主义已经消亡，支持和反对它的论点已经陈旧而陈旧。它的本质已经改变了。随着中产阶级的消失，下层阶级不断受到挤压，亿万富翁成为万亿富翁，人们以低工资为直接原因纵火焚烧仓库，总统下令自动党卫军登记，因为他威胁要彻底摧毁文明，它已经转移，完全变成了其他东西。

这狗屎不起作用，而且只会变得更糟。

Look around you, the economy is aligning itself entirely around gambling. From bitcoin to nfts to the stock market to AI to art to dating apps to social media feeds to video games to venture capital to literal gambling apps infesting our phones ads sports. And finally we have actual members of the government gambling on policy.

The capitalism you grew up with is dead, the arguments for and against it are old and stale. That nature of what it is has changed. It's metastasized, devolved into something else entirely as the middle class is evaporating, the lower class is continually squeezed, billionaires become trillionaires, people are lighting warehouses on fire citing low wages as the proximal cause, and the President is ordering automatic SS registration as he threatens total civilization destruction.

This shit is not working and it's only going to get worse.

在 HN 中查看
pigpop 2026/04/11 22:53
这些是道德病，而不是资本主义病。因为工资不够而放火烧仓库的人是不道德的人。在共产主义国家，他们会被称为“破坏者”，并会因为他们的行为而面临行刑队。

同样，赌博也是一个道德问题。它应该是非法的或受到严格限制的，而且经常如此。我们现在面临的许多其他问题可以通过恢复过去存在的法律来解决。

Those are diseases of morality, not capitalism. Someone who lights a warehouse on fire because they aren't paid enough is an immoral person. In a communist country they would be called a Wrecker and they'd face a firing squad for their actions.

Gambling, likewise, is a moral problem. It should be illegal or highly restricted and often was. Many other problems we face now could be fixed simply by reinstating laws that used to exist.

在 HN 中查看
ModernMech 2026/04/12 02:21
你和我对于什么是道德的、什么是不道德的问题可能有非常不同的看法。对我来说，最高的道德犯罪是贪婪。今天很多人似乎都对此表示满意。因此，如果你说火灾的发生是因为放火者的道德品质差，那么我也可以很容易地说，这是因为那些没有支付他合理工资的人的道德品质差而发生的，这让我们无处可去。

这就是为什么我专注于纯粹的系统性论证。系统中的人是抽象的。无论他们是否道德，他们都会对系统性激励做出反应。那么，如果按照你的说法，他们的行为是不道德的，为什么这个制度会鼓励堕落行为而不是道德行为呢？

You and I probably have very different ideas about what is moral and what is not. For me, the highest moral crime is greed. A lot of people seem fine with it today. So for you to claim that the fire happened because of the poor moral character of the person who lit the fire, I can just as easily say this happened because of the poor moral character of the people who didn't pay him a fair wage, which leaves us nowhere.

That's why I focus on purely systemic arguments. The humans in the system are abstract. It doesn't matter if they are moral or not, they respond to systemic incentives. So if they're acting immorally according to you, why is the system incentivizing degenerate behavior instead of moral behavior?

miki123211 2026/04/11 23:16
私人监狱的概念基本上证明了为什么不了解资本主义的人不应该参与其中。

资本主义相对于社会主义的全部优势在于，客户的选择迫使你生产更好的产品，其他一切都源于此。如果你在城里只有一家杂货店、一套学校系统、一家医疗保险公司、一位雇主，那么无论他们变得多么糟糕，你都将永远是他们的奴隶。如果这些机构的决策者没有从机构的良好表现中获利，情况尤其如此。

在私人监狱系统中，别无选择，如果你不喜欢自己所在的监狱，你就不能换监狱。这是所有系统中最糟糕的，因为仍然存在利润激励，而没有竞争对其进行控制。

我认为你可以把私人监狱系统做得很好。也许让囚犯选择他们的监狱，如果囚犯越狱，监狱主人就会受到重大抑制。也许可以为新释放的囚犯出狱后的每一天向他们支付报酬，从而鼓励真正有效的康复计划。这些将使对所有者有利的事情与对社会有利的事情结合起来，这就是资本主义的最终目的。

The concept of private prisons basically proves why people who don't understand capitalism shouldn't engage in it.

The entire advantage of capitalism over socialism is that customer choice forces you to make better products, and everything else stems from that. If you only have one grocery store, one school system, one health insurer, one employer in town, it doesn't matter how bad they get, you'll be their slave forever. This is particularly true if the person making the decisions at those institutions doesn't profit from them doing well.

In a private prison system, there's no choice, you can't just switch prisons if you don't like the one you're in. This is the worst system of all, as there's still the profit incentive, without competition keeping it in check.

I think you could do a private prison system well. Maybe let the prisoners choose their prison, with major disincentives for prison owners if prisoners escape. Maybe pay them for every day their newly-released inmates stay out of jail, encouraging rehab programs that actually work. Those would align what's good for the owner with what's good for society, which is all that capitalism is ultimately all about.

在 HN 中查看
ModernMech 2026/04/11 21:59
资本主义国家没有饥荒 在家里。这与“资本主义防止饥荒”不同。如果对资本家有利的话，资本主义很乐意造成饥饿、造成死亡或监禁人们，这已融入到体系结构中。资本主义不存在指导资本生成活动的系统性特征 除非 它侵犯了自然权利和人权。
Capitalist countries don't have famines at home. That's different from "capitalism prevents famines". Capitalism is happy to cause hunger, inflict death, or imprison people if it's profitable for capitalists, that's baked into the structure of the structure of the system. There's no systemic feature of capitalism that directs capital generating activity unless it violates natural and human rights.
